data.description:
## Why
Job 1: refute an asserted absence. When a reviewer (plan or code) claims an import/symbol is hallucinated, this
lane tries to DISPROVE it by resolving the reference against the repo — the highest-value false-positive reducer
for hallucinated-symbol findings. It is confirm-only: `refuted` (found it) or `abstain`, never asserts absence.

## What
- A **deterministic T1 resolver** over a full repo checkout via grep-ast / universal-ctags (tree-sitter,
  pip-installable, no server, no build): a repo-wide symbol/definition index + plain file-path existence.
- Resolution outcomes: file-path exists (deterministic), symbol present in a cleanly-parsed file (authoritative),
  import / cross-file resolution (confirm-only → `refuted`/`abstain`, never `absent`).
- The **explicit reference-in schema** the review agent must emit (`{kind, name, in_file?, ...}`) — the
  integration contract. Optional deterministic extraction FROM a diff/file is in scope; prose extraction is NOT.
- Tiered design: grep-ast/ctags is the always-available floor. LSP/SCIP semantic resolution is **T2 — out of
  scope here**, deferred to `850f` (servers/builds, non-deterministic).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/grounding/resolve.py` (+ helpers); reuse `grep-ast`/`ctags`; build on S1's contract + harness.
- Avoid stack-graphs (archived). No language servers, no builds (those are T2).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `refute_absence(reference)` returns `refuted` (with location) or `abstain(reason)` — never asserts absence.
- [ ] Polyglot repo-wide symbol/file index via grep-ast/ctags; per-language coverage recorded; unsupported lang → abstain.
- [ ] Explicit reference-in schema defined + validated; optional deterministic code/diff reference extractor.
- [ ] Fail-open tested (no tool / parse error / timeout / unsupported lang → abstain) on a polyglot fixture set.
data.title: Engine A — refutation resolver (grep-ast/ctags T1 floor) + reference-in schema
